name: Italian Game
description: A classical opening aiming for rapid development and control of the center. Bc4 targets the f7 pawn.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1c4

name: Italian Game: Giuoco Piano
description: After Bc4 Bc5, both sides develop naturally. White aims for d4 to open the center.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1c4 f8c5 c2c3

name: Ruy Lopez
description: One of the oldest and most respected openings. Bb5 puts pressure on the knight defending e5.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1b5

name: Ruy Lopez: Morphy Defense
description: After a6, White usually retreats Ba4. The main line leads to complex strategic play.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1b5 a7a6 f5a4 g8f6 e1g1

name: Scotch Game
description: An aggressive alternative to the quiet Italian. White immediately strikes in the center with d4.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4

name: Vienna Game
description: Nc3 before Nf3, keeping options open for an f4 pawn push (Vienna Gambit).
moves: e2e4 e7e5 b1c3

name: King's Gambit
description: A romantic-era gambit offering the f-pawn for rapid development and open lines toward the king.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 f2f4

name: Queen's Gambit
description: The most popular d4 opening. White offers the c-pawn to gain central control.
moves: d2d4 d7d5 c2c4

name: Queen's Gambit: Exchange Variation
description: After cxd5, White aims for a minority attack on the queenside.
moves: d2d4 d7d5 c2c4 e7e6 b1c3 g8f6 c4d5 e6d5

name: London System
description: A solid, low-theory system. Bf4 before e3 creates a sturdy pawn structure.
moves: d2d4 d7d5 c1f4 g8f6 e2e3 e7e6 b1d2

name: King's Indian Attack
description: A flexible system where White fianchettoes the kingside bishop and castles before committing to a pawn structure.
moves: g1f3 d7d5 g2g3 g8f6 f1g2 e7e6 e1g1

name: English Opening
description: A flank opening that controls the center from the side. Transposes into many other openings.
moves: c2c4

name: Catalan Opening
description: Combines d4 and c4 with a kingside fianchetto. The g2 bishop exerts long-term pressure on the queenside.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 e7e6 g2g3 d7d5 f1g2

name: Four Knights Game
description: A symmetrical opening where both sides develop knights first. Leads to balanced positions.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 b1c3 g8f6

name: Bishop's Opening
description: Develops the bishop early to c4 before the knight. Can transpose into the Italian or Vienna.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 f1c4

name: Sicilian Defense: Open
description: The most popular response to 1.e4. Creates asymmetric positions with chances for both sides.
moves: e2e4 c7c5

name: Sicilian Defense: Najdorf Variation
description: The most theoretically dense Sicilian line. a6 prevents Bb5 and prepares e5 or b5 expansions.
moves: e2e4 c7c5 g1f3 d7d6 d2d4 c5d4 f3d4 g8f6 b1c3 a7a6

name: Sicilian Defense: Dragon Variation
description: Black fianchettoes the dark-squared bishop, aiming it at the center and queenside. Leads to exciting play.
moves: e2e4 c7c5 g1f3 d7d6 d2d4 c5d4 f3d4 g8f6 b1c3 g7g6

name: French Defense
description: A solid defense creating a pawn chain. Black's light-squared bishop is often blocked but the structure is resilient.
moves: e2e4 e7e6

name: French Defense: Advance Variation
description: After e5, Black attacks the pawn chain base with c5 and gains queenside counterplay.
moves: e2e4 e7e6 d2d4 d7d5 e4e5 c7c5

name: Caro-Kann Defense
description: A solid alternative to the French. c6 supports d5 without blocking the light-squared bishop.
moves: e2e4 c7c6

name: Caro-Kann: Classical Variation
description: After exd5 cxd5 Nc3, Black develops Bf5 before e6, solving the light-squared bishop problem.
moves: e2e4 c7c6 d2d4 d7d5 b1c3 d5e4 c3e4 c8f5

name: Scandinavian Defense
description: An aggressive response to 1.e4 — Black immediately challenges the center pawn.
moves: e2e4 d7d5

name: Pirc Defense
description: A hypermodern defense where Black allows White to build a center, planning to undermine it later.
moves: e2e4 d7d6 d2d4 g8f6 b1c3 g7g6

name: King's Indian Defense
description: Black allows White a broad center, then counterattacks with e5 or c5. Highly dynamic play.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7

name: King's Indian: Classical Variation
description: Both sides develop fully before the central tension breaks. Black aims for e5 and kingside play.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 f8g7 e2e4 d7d6 g1f3 e8g8 f1e2 e7e5

name: Nimzo-Indian Defense
description: One of Black's most respected defenses against d4. Bb4 pins the knight and fights for e4 control.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 e7e6 b1c3 f8b4

name: Queen's Indian Defense
description: If White avoids Nc3, Black plays b6 and Bb7 for long-diagonal control.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 e7e6 g1f3 b7b6

name: Queen's Gambit Declined
description: Black solidly defends d5 with e6. A classical approach leading to rich middlegame play.
moves: d2d4 d7d5 c2c4 e7e6

name: Slav Defense
description: c6 supports d5 while keeping the light-squared bishop unblocked. Combines solidity with flexibility.
moves: d2d4 d7d5 c2c4 c7c6

name: Grunfeld Defense
description: Black gives up the center early, then attacks it with c5 and the fianchettoed bishop. Sharp and dynamic.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 g7g6 b1c3 d7d5

name: Dutch Defense
description: An aggressive response to d4. f5 fights for e4 control but weakens the kingside.
moves: d2d4 f7f5

name: Benoni Defense
description: Black plays c5 early against d4, creating an asymmetric pawn structure with queenside play.
moves: d2d4 g8f6 c2c4 c7c5 d4d5 e7e6

name: Alekhine Defense
description: A provocative defense — the knight invites White to advance pawns, then attacks the overextended center.
moves: e2e4 g8f6

name: Petroff Defense
description: A symmetrical response to 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3. Black immediately counterattacks the e4 pawn.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 g8f6

name: Philidor Defense
description: d6 supports e5 solidly. A reliable but somewhat passive defense.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 d7d6

name: Italian Game: Two Knights Defense
description: A sharp response to the Italian. Black develops Nf6 attacking e4 instead of the quieter Bc5.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1c4 g8f6

name: Ruy Lopez: Berlin Defense
description: Made famous by Kramnik vs Kasparov. The endgame after Bxc6 dxc6 Nxe5 is solid for Black.
moves: e2e4 e7e5 g1f3 b8c6 f1b5 g8f6

name: Sicilian Defense: Accelerated Dragon
description: Black plays g6 before d6, aiming for the Maroczy Bind structure with possible d5 break.
moves: e2e4 c7c5 g1f3 b8c6 d2d4 c5d4 f3d4 g7g6

name: English Defense
description: A flexible response to d4/c4 with b6, fianchettoing the queen's bishop early.
moves: d2d4 e7e6 c2c4 b7b6
